receiver_rule: These metrics describe internal realism baseline, not outcome calibration. They cannot be used to downgrade claim ceilings or justify public-launch language. council_decision_quality remains descriptive_only until v0b verdict-outcome alignment is established.

language_boundary.maximum_honest_claim: council_decision_quality has moved from purely descriptive to 'trackable realism baseline with persistence capability established'

agreement_stability.interpretation: dominant_ratio near 1.0 = highly stable; jsd near 0.0 = low distribution drift across halves
agreement_stability.measures: vote distribution consistency for the same input across sessions
agreement_stability.does_not_measure: whether the verdict was correct or appropriate

internal_self_consistency.interpretation: consistency_rate near 1.0 = internal signals align with verdict direction; low rate suggests the council produces contradictory signal-verdict pairs
internal_self_consistency.measures: alignment between internal signals (tension, objection, coherence) and verdict direction

suppression_recovery_rate.interpretation: recovery_rate near 0.0 = minority positions never recover; moderate rate suggests the council can self-correct on repeated input
suppression_recovery_rate.measures: rate at which suppressed minority positions later become the dominant verdict
suppression_recovery_rate.does_not_measure: whether the minority position was correct

persistence_coverage.interpretation: overall near 1.0 = all required fields present in all records; gaps indicate persistence schema drift or partial writes
persistence_coverage.measures: field completeness of persisted council verdict records

v0b_prerequisites.note: v0b is not in scope for V1.1; these fields track forward readiness only
